title: Superintendent
notes: Rural ~250-student district; no dedicated ECE/curriculum director. Cosby was named superintendent in 2023 after long tenure as coach/AD. District email domain is cplains.esc14.net (Region 14 ESC host) even though public site is crossplainsisd.net. Cross Plains Elementary was named a Blue Ribbon School in 2023.

title: Superintendent
notes: Big Sandy-area East TX district (~700 students); no dedicated ECE/curriculum director surfaced. Note: Alphin announced resignation in late April 2026 per Gilmer Mirror joint statement -- contact may be in transition. Distinct from Harmony Public Schools charter network.

title: Superintendent
notes: Dr. Bobby G. Rice serves as Superintendent of Harts Bluff ISD (Titus County, Mount Pleasant area). District has no dedicated ECE or curriculum director listed. Email not published on public site; format appears to be [first-initial][last]@hbisd.net based on other staff (e.g., ovallek@hbisd.net) -- enrich via Apollo.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Panhandle district (~100 students); superintendent is the only viable ECE/curriculum contact. Phone ext. 22.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small rural district (~260 students, Fisher/Scurry counties); no dedicated ECE/curriculum director. Domain is the Region 14 ESC host (hermleigh.esc14.net). Ellerbe hired as superintendent for 2025-26.

title: Executive Director of Curriculum, Instruction, and Assessment
notes: Dallas-area HPISD (~7,000 students). Amy Wood leads Curriculum, Instruction, and Assessment within Education Services dept; oversees PreK High-Quality Preschool Program. Email inferred from district [first].[last] pattern -- public site uses an emailer form rather than displaying address. Confidence Medium because format is the secondary HPISD pattern; primary is [last][first_initial]@hpisd.org (e.g., woodam@hpisd.org). Verify pattern in Apollo. HPISD ELA curriculum review ongoing per district news.

title: Assistant Superintendent
notes: Holliday ISD (~1,100 students, Archer County). Booher oversees curriculum development, staff development, special programs (Sp Ed, 504, ELL). Superintendent is Cody Carroll (cody.carroll@hollidayisd.net) -- can be backup contact.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small Erath County district (~345 students); superintendent since 2022. No dedicated ECE/curriculum director. Email inferred from common [first-initial][last]@hisd.us pattern confirmed by ZoomInfo prefix 'w***@hisd.us'. Backup district email: info@hisd.us.

title: Chief Academic Officer of Curriculum & Instruction
notes: Ingleside ISD (San Patricio County, ~2,000 students). Ceballos is the curriculum lead. Superintendent: Scott Kilgore. Special Ed Director: Yolanda Alvaro.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Bosque County PK-12 district (~140 students). Morrow doubles as Title IX Coordinator and instructional leadership. No dedicated ECE staff -- elementary principal Bonnie Johnston (b_johnston@iredell-isd.com) is alternate contact.

title: Executive Director of Teaching & Learning
notes: Fast-growing Williamson County district (~6,000+ students). Dr. Patty Cote leads Teaching & Learning dept overseeing curriculum. Email inferred from district [first].[last]@jarrellisd.org pattern (confirmed by Maria Cuellar, Federal Programs Director). Superintendent: Dr. Toni Hicks. District is in growth mode -- strong PreK expansion signal.

title: Superintendent
notes: Blanco County rural district (~700 students); 5th year as superintendent. Website notes new domain johnsoncity.txed.net replacing jc.txed.net. No dedicated ECE/curriculum director surfaced.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small Harrison County PK-8 district (~125 students). Fitzpatrick named superintendent Aug 2023; previous 18 years in Marshall ISD. No dedicated ECE/curriculum role -- this is the only viable contact.

title: Superintendent/Principal
notes: Tiny PK-6 county-wide district based in Sarita (~50 students); Tinsley serves dual super/principal role. Note: public site domain is saritaschool.net but email domain is saritaschool.com (per district staff page).

title: Director of Curriculum and Instruction
notes: Kingsville ISD (Kleberg County, ~3,400 students). Dr. Ballesteros is the primary curriculum lead. Asst Supt C&I: Dr. Joseph Welch (jwelch@kingsvilleisd.com) is backup. Email inferred from confirmed district [first-initial][last]@kingsvilleisd.com pattern.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Bosque County PK-12 district (~225 students). Adcock also handles non-discrimination/Title IX inquiries. No dedicated ECE/curriculum director.

title: Assistant Superintendent
notes: La Grange ISD (Fayette County, ~2,000 students). Eilers oversees curriculum/academics as AsstSupt. Superintendent: Matthew Andy McHazlett. Nicole Vasut is Federal Programs Director (alternate ECE/Title I contact).

title: Assistant Superintendent of Teaching & Learning
notes: Lago Vista ISD (Travis County, ~1,800 students). Pasak owns curriculum and instruction. Deputy Supt: Dr. Suzy Lofton-Bullis. Strategic Plan 2022-2032 in place.

title: Early Childhood Coordinator
notes: Lancaster ISD (Dallas County, ~6,000 students). Glynn is the named ECE expert -- updates curriculum, runs classroom observations, manages interagency agreements. Strong buying signal: Lancaster ISD ECE educators received Texas School Ready grant. Asst Supt of Elementary: Dr. Helena Mosely (helena.mosely@lancasterisd.org) is the higher-level decision-maker. Email format [first].[last]@lancasterisd.org used by 92% of district emails.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Parmer/Castro County PK-12 district (~130 students); 9:1 student-teacher ratio. District of Innovation -- offers teacher housing and employee childcare (strong ECE program signal). No dedicated curriculum director.

title: Director of Elementary Curriculum & Instruction
notes: Levelland ISD (Hockley County, ~2,800 students). Runkles is the NEW Director of Elementary C&I as of March 2026 restructuring after Donna Pugh's retirement. Strong buying signal: Levelland ISD recently transitioned to Bluebonnet Learning curriculum (West Texas Watchdogs reported March 2026). Dr. Matthew Birdwell handles secondary C&I.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Erath County PK-12 district. No dedicated curriculum/ECE director. Brian Sanders is Principal (backup contact).

title: Director of Elementary / Director of Bilingual & ESL
notes: Lockhart ISD (Caldwell County, ~5,400 students). Vasquez is the primary elementary curriculum/instruction lead within C&I dept; also leads Bilingual/ESL. Reports to Ty Davidson, Asst Supt of Curriculum (ty.davidson@lockhart.txed.net). Note district email domain is lockhart.txed.net, separate from public site lockhartisd.org.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small Gaines/Terry County rural district (~143 students). Blackmon handles employment inquiries directly. No dedicated ECE/curriculum director.

title: Superintendent
notes: Louise ISD (Wharton County, ~520 students). Wright started Sept 2024 (3-year contract through June 2027), replacing Garth Oliver. Asst Supt/HR: Pam Wagner. Elementary Principal (PK-5): Lori Heard -- ECE-relevant backup contact.

title: Superintendent
notes: Lyford CISD (Willacy County, ~1,500 students). Board named 2022 TX Outstanding School Board. Victoria Zamora is Special Education Director. No dedicated ECE/curriculum director surfaced; superintendent is the strongest contact.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Brewster County K-12 district (~80 students) in rural Big Bend. Dr. Durant's email per Texas data records. Pre-K teacher: Melissa Barlow. No dedicated curriculum role.

title: Superintendent
notes: Mart ISD (McLennan County, ~600 students). Hayward took role mid-2025 after board terminated prior super Betsy Burnett (Nov 2024 board vote; TEA examiner ruled good cause in March 2025). Note email domain is mymartisd.org, not martisd.org. Recent leadership churn = potential opening signal.

title: Interim Superintendent
notes: McCamey ISD (Upton County, ~530 students). Dr. Schneider is interim; 34+ years experience including 20+ as superintendent. District hosted on Region 18 ESC (mcisd.esc18.net). Strong recent bond passage for teacher housing -- growth/staffing signal.

title: Superintendent
notes: McMullen County ISD (~280 students, rural South Texas). Jones is sole administrative decision-maker. No dedicated ECE/curriculum staff.

title: Superintendent
notes: Meridian ISD (Bosque County, ~430 students). Davis is sole admin contact; no dedicated ECE/curriculum director.

title: Superintendent
notes: Milano ISD (Milam County, ~400 students). Terry was named superintendent Jan 2026 after serving as Acting Super following hospitalization/death of Dr. Elisabeth Avila Luevanos in Oct 2025. 26 years at Milano ISD. Email inferred from district [first].[last]@milanoisd.net pattern. Backup: milanoisd@milanoisd.net. Recent leadership change = transition signal.

title: Superintendent
notes: MWP ISD (Ward County, ~2,200 students). Dr. Parker previously held Executive Director of Academics and Student Services role -- direct curriculum/instruction experience. District hosted on Region 18 ESC (mwpisd.esc18.net).

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Shackelford County district (~115 students). Dr. Denson confirmed as superintendent via LinkedIn and TASA. Email inferred from district pattern on Region 14 ESC domain -- ZoomInfo confirms moran.esc14.net domain. No dedicated curriculum role.

title: Superintendent
notes: Moulton ISD (Lavaca/Gonzales counties, small ~340 student district). Dr. Fairchild is superintendent; oversees PK-12 with two campuses. Email inferred from common [first-initial][last]@moultonisd.net pattern -- verify in Apollo. Backup: HS principal Jamie Dornak.

title: Superintendent
notes: Mumford ISD (Robertson County, ~600 students). Mumford Elementary named Blue Ribbon School in 2018. District serves PK3 through grade 12 across 4 buildings -- strong early childhood program presence.

title: Superintendent
notes: Neches ISD (Anderson County, ~370 students). Tiner is 2025-26 superintendent. Email inferred from common [first-initial][last]@nechesisd.com pattern -- verify in Apollo. No dedicated curriculum director.

title: Superintendent
notes: New Summerfield ISD (Cherokee County, ~510 students). 4-day school week. Note district email domain is newsummerfieldisd.net (not .org). District improved TEA rating from C to A in 1 year -- strong academics/curriculum signal.

title: Director of Special Programs
notes: Nocona ISD (Montague County, ~860 students). Wright handles special programs including curriculum-adjacent areas. Superintendent: David Waters (dwaters@noconaisd.net). Note: there is a Stephenie Wright also tied to Nocona HS principal -- confirm current role before outreach.

title: Superintendent
notes: Tiny Victoria County K-7 district (~75 students); 6th year as superintendent. District recently expanded to grades 6-7 and proposing 9-12 pathway via partnership with Victoria ISD -- growth signal. Email inferred from common [first-initial][last]@nurseryisd.org pattern -- verify in Apollo. Principal: Jennifer Southern.
